subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.1.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of God as the Creator
indicator_description: Examine our relationship with the Creator
examplars:
1. Learners talk about God's creation using pictures and charts and real things from the environment.
2. Learners listen to creation stories/watch videos from the internet about creation.
3. Learners role play to retell the creation story.
4. Learners draw, colour, make models, recite rhymes, sing songs about God's creation: human beings, animals, trees, rivers, moon, stars, sea and mountains.
5. Learners discuss and role play the attributes of God.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.1.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of self as a unique individual
indicator_description: Recognise individual characteristics as the basis of one's uniqueness
examplars:
1. Learners in groups identify and talk about their individual characteristics and accept themselves, bringing out their similarities and differences.
2. Learners brainstorm on the importance of knowing and appreciating oneself.
3. Learners say things about themselves with confidence and positive self-esteem.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.1.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of one's relationship with others and the need for good interpersonal relationships
indicator_description: Identify persons closely related us and the need to relate well with others
examplars:
1. Learners mention the names of those who are closely related to them, e.g. parents, brothers, sisters, friends and teachers.
2. Learners in groups, talk about the importance of inter-personal relationships and the need to accept everyone, irrespective of where the person comes from.
3. Learners role play healthy relationship with family and friends.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.1.4.1. Demonstrate understanding of the place you call home
indicator_description: Describe and show direction to their home
examplars:
1. Learners take turns to talk about their home e.g. my home is in a good and beautiful neighbourhood.
2. Learners sketch the direction to their home on paper.
3. Learners talk about what they want to add to their home.
4. Learners draw their home.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.2.1.1 Demonstrate knowledge of things in the environment
indicator_description: Explore the uses of things in the environment
examplars:
1. Engage learners in a nature walk to observe, identify, locate and talk about things in the environment and their uses e.g. Furniture: chairs, tables, cupboards, Plants, Animals, Computers and sort them into living, non-living, manmade and natural.
2. Learners browse the internet, draw or look at pictures or posters of things in the environment and talk about caring for them in environment as responsible citizens.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.2.1.2 Demonstrate knowledge of different types of weather conditions
indicator_description: Identify and describe different weather conditions
examplars:
1. Show pictures/films of different weather conditions.
2. Learners use rhymes, e.g. 'rain, rain go away', to tell the types of weather e.g. i) Sunny weather, ii) Windy weather, iii) Rainy weather, iv) Cloudy weather.
3. Learners in groups: describe the weather type from their pictures to the class; mention the weather of the day from what they can observe outside their classroom.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.2.2.1. Recognise different plants in the environment
indicator_description: Identify different plants in the environments and their uses
examplars:
1. Learners go on a nature walk to observe and talk about different plants in the environment.
2. Learner watch films/pictures of plants in the environment to enable learners understand that different plants live in different environments.
3. Learners talk about uses of plants and share the information with the whole class.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.2.2.2. Recognise and appreciate different animals in the environment
indicator_description: Identify different animals in the environments and their uses
examplars:
1. Use school and community environments, pictures, films from the internet, to show different animals such as fish, birds, monkeys, butterflies, ants to enable learners to understand that different animals live in different environments.
2. Learners draw fishes in rivers, birds in their nests, pets in their sleeping places in learners' homes.
3. Learners collect and share information about animals from the internet, parents, books.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.2.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of the cardinal points
indicator_description: Identify the cardinal points
examplars:
1. Learners talk about the cardinal points e.g. North, South, East and West.
2. Learners sketch the cardinal points and label them.
3. Learners stretch their arms sideways to demonstrate the cardinal points e.g. in front of me is North, to the right is East, to the left is West and to my back is South.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.2.4.1. Demonstrate basic understanding of population structure (age and sex structure)
indicator_description: Describe the population structure of their class and the need to respect one another
examplars:
1. Learners in groups describe the structure of population in their class by sex and age.
2. Learners group the population in their class by age and by sex in their class.
3. Learners talk about the need to respect one another regardless of age and sex as responsible citizens.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.3.1.1. Appreciate the importance of religious worship
indicator_description: Explore the main types of worship in Ghana
examplars:
1. Show pictures, video clips, etc. of people worshipping, among the three main religions in Ghana.
2. Learners role play the act of worship in the three main religions in Ghana.
3. Learners sing and recite texts from the three main religions.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.3.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of festivals in Ghana
indicator_description: Describe religious festivals in Ghana
examplars:
1. Learners mention festivals celebrated in the three religions in Ghana.
2. Learners role play religious tolerance e.g. prayers at the Independence Day celebrations, celebrations of major religious festivals.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.3.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of human rights as a citizen
indicator_description: Explain basic human needs and rights of self and others
examplars:
1. Engage learners with games/story telling, rhymes to talk about human needs and rights.
2. Human needs: food, shelter. Clothing. Human rights: right to life, freedom of association, right to movement, personal liberty, right to fair trial, etc.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.3.4.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the birth stories of religious leaders
indicator_description: Narrate the stories of the birth of religious leaders
examplars:
1. Show pictures and video clips depicting the birth of the religious leaders: Christian, Islam and African Traditional Religion (ATR).
2. Learners tell stories about the birth of the religious leaders.
3. Learners role play the birth of the religious leaders.
4. Project Work: Learners to consult their parents and record their dates and town of birth.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.4.1.1. Demonstrate Understanding of the characteristics of a responsible citizen
indicator_description: Mention the characteristics of a responsible citizen
examplars:
1. Learners talk about who a responsible citizen is, e.g. obeying parents and school rules, respecting people, taking care of school property, keeping the environment tidy, etc.
2. Learners role play on some of the characteristics of a responsible citizen.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.4.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of obeying power and authority as a responsible citizen
indicator_description: Explore sources of power and authority
examplars:
1. Learners identify the national symbols and appreciate them: National Flag, Anthem, Pledge, Coat of Arms.
2. Learners, in groups/pairs, tell how these national symbols can be preserved.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.4.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of obeying power and authority as a responsible citizen
indicator_description: Identify people who have power and authority and respect them, as a responsible citizen
examplars:
1. Learners talk about the people who have power and authority: at home, in class, in the community, and in the nation.
2. Learners give examples of persons occupying such positions.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.4.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of the importance of energy in our environment
indicator_description: Explore the importance of energy in the home, school and community
examplars:
1. Through questions and answers, learners mention the types of energy sources available, e.g. sun, wind, firewood, charcoal, kerosene and gas.
2. Learners talk about uses of energy, e.g. cooking, smoking and drying of food items.
3. Learners draw a bulb, flashlight coal pot, etc.
4. Share their drawings with peers in the class.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.4.4.1. Demonstrate understanding of farming as an important activity
indicator_description: Describe farming activities in the community
examplars:
1. Learners look around the school or community and talk about different farm activities.
2. Learners draw some farming activities e.g. weeding the farm or garden, feeding animals.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.4.4.2. Show understanding of simple agricultural tools use in Ghana
indicator_description: Identify simple agricultural tools
examplars:
1. Learners look at pictures of simple agricultural tools or the real tools people use in the community to farm.
2. Draw some of the agricultural tools used in Ghana, e.g. cutlass, hoe, mattock, rake, hand fork, axe, watering can, etc.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.5.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of Ghana's Neighbouring Countries
indicator_description: Mention Ghana's Neighbours
examplars:
1. Learners identify their unique country, Ghana and her neighbours.
2. Learners demonstrate the positions of Ghana's neighbouring countries by using the body.
3. Learners compose a song/rhyme and draw a learner with arm stretched showing Ghana's neighbours.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.5.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of the parts of a computer
indicator_description: Identify parts of a computer and how they connect to each other
examplars:
1. Learners talk about the parts of a computer e.g. mouse.
2. Learners work in small groups to explore how the gadgets connect to each other.
3. Guide learners to tell how the gadgets are connected.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.5.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of data and sources of information
indicator_description: Collect types of data
examplars:
1. Guide learners to collect various items from the environment, count and record them in a book.
2. Talk about the various types of data gathered.

subject: Our World and Our People
content_standard: B1.5.4.1. Recognise technology tools in communication
indicator_description: Identify technology tools in communication
examplars:
1. Learners in groups talk about technology tools used for communication e.g. gong gong, drums, bells, mobile vans, and community information centres.
2. Learners draw and colour drums, bells, gong gong to assemble people.
